title: Recovering from a k3s Traefik port hijack
description: A step-by-step incident walkthrough: how k3s's bundled Traefik LoadBalancer can steal ports 80/443 from Caddy, and how to fix it.

steps.title: Recognize the symptoms
steps.content:
All Caddy-fronted domains return `404 Not Found` with the **TRAEFIK DEFAULT CERT**. Your apps are healthy when checked directly on their localhost ports, but public HTTPS fails uniformly.

This happens because k3s's bundled Traefik is exposed as a `LoadBalancer`, and k3s's ServiceLB (Klipper) pod DNATs host `:80/:443` traffic into the cluster before it reaches Caddy.

steps.title: Confirm who is listening on 80/443
steps.content: Caddy may still hold the socket, but packets can be redirected below it by iptables. Check both the socket owner and the firewall rules.
steps.aiHint: If you see 'traefik' or 'klipper' here, the k3s ServiceLB pod is binding the host network ports directly.

steps.title: Confirm iptables DNAT rules
steps.content: ServiceLB inserts iptables rules that rewrite packets destined for the host's public IP on `:80/:443` to the cluster's internal Traefik service. Even if Caddy owns the socket, the packet never arrives.
steps.aiHint: If you see DNAT rules pointing to 10.43.x.x addresses, ServiceLB is actively hijacking the ports. An empty result means the hijack is not present.

steps.title: Inspect the k3s ServiceLB pods
steps.content: k3s creates a host-network DaemonSet called `svclb-traefik-*` for each Traefik LoadBalancer service. These pods bind the host ports.
steps.aiHint: If pods are Running, they are holding the host ports. Deleting the Traefik HelmChart and LoadBalancer service will remove them.

steps.title: Disable Traefik in k3s config
steps.content:
Create or edit `/etc/rancher/k3s/config.yaml`:

```yaml
disable:
  - traefik
```

Then restart k3s:

```bash
sudo systemctl restart k3s
```
steps.aiHint: If the config file does not exist or does not contain 'disable: [traefik]', the next k3s restart may re-enable Traefik.

steps.title: Remove leftover Traefik resources
steps.content:
Delete the HelmChart so k3s does not recreate Traefik, then delete the LoadBalancer service and its svclb DaemonSet:

```bash
kubectl delete helmchart -n kube-system traefik
kubectl delete svc -n kube-system traefik
kubectl delete daemonset -n kube-system svclb-traefik
```
steps.aiHint: If the service still exists, ServiceLB may still be running. The expected result is 'Error from server (NotFound)'.

steps.title: Verify the edge is free
steps.content: After k3s settles, confirm no DNAT rules remain and Caddy is serving the real certificates again.
steps.checkCommand: sudo iptables -t nat -L -n | grep -E ':80|:443' && curl -I -k https://cloudie.serendepify.com/
steps.aiHint: If the curl still shows a Traefik certificate, look for stale DNAT rules or another LoadBalancer service in kube-system.
